content.problemStatement:
Write a program that takes two space separated inputs - the age (an integer) and the name of country(a string) and does the following:


Prints "Eligible" if the age is greater than or equal to `18` and country is **India**
Prints "Not Eligible", otherwise.
Check the sample input / output below for further clarity.
constraints: country consists only of alphabetic characters
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains an integer age and a string country separated by a single space.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "Eligible" if age >= 18 and country is exactly "India", otherwise output "Not Eligible".

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: age 21 >= 18 and country India → Eligible. Test case 2: age 16 < 18 → Not Eligible. Test case 3: country Nepal ≠ India → Not Eligible.
First line meets both conditions, second line fails country check.
Age 0 is less than 18, so not eligible.

solution.approach: Read the number of test cases. For each test case read the integer age and the country string. Check two conditions: age must be at least 18 and the country string must be exactly "India" (case‑sensitive). If both hold print "Eligible" otherwise print "Not Eligible". The algorithm is O(T) time and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – each test case is processed in constant time.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used regardless of T.
